name: Array
definition: Contiguous block of memory storing fixed-size elements, accessed by integer index in O(1).
time: Access O(1) · Search O(n) · Insert/Delete O(n)
techniques: Two pointers; Sliding window; Prefix sum; Difference array; In-place swap; Sorting
notes: Most common interview structure. Master sliding window and two pointers first.

leetcode.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

name: Dynamic Array (ArrayList / Vector)
definition: Resizable array that doubles capacity on overflow giving amortized O(1) append.
time: Access O(1) · Append amortized O(1) · Insert/Delete O(n)
notes: Java ArrayList, C++ vector, Python list.

name: String
definition: Immutable (in most languages) sequence of characters; an array of chars under the hood.
time: Access O(1) · Concat O(n) · Substring O(n)
techniques: Two pointers; Sliding window; Hashing; Trie; KMP; Rabin-Karp
notes: Use StringBuilder/list-join in Python/Java to avoid O(n^2) concatenation.

name: Linked List (Singly)
definition: Chain of nodes, each holding a value and a pointer to the next node.
time: Access O(n) · Insert/Delete at head O(1)
techniques: Two pointers; Fast & slow (Floyd's cycle); Dummy head; Iterative reverse
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If given a linked list → Two pointers.'

leetcode.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List

name: Doubly Linked List
definition: Linked list where each node has prev and next pointers, enabling O(1) deletion given a node.
time: Access O(n) · Insert/Delete given node O(1)
notes: Backbone of LRU cache (combined with hash map).

leetcode.title: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List

name: Stack
definition: LIFO container supporting push and pop at the top in O(1).
techniques: Recursion simulation; Monotonic stack; Parentheses matching; Function call tracking
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If recursion is banned → Stack.'

name: Queue
definition: FIFO container; enqueue at the rear, dequeue at the front in O(1).
notes: Used everywhere in BFS.

name: Deque (Double-ended queue)
definition: Container with O(1) insert/remove at both ends; basis of monotonic queue.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for sliding window max/min → Monotonic queue.'

leetcode.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K
Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit

name: Hash Table / Hash Map
definition: Key→value store using a hash function to map keys to buckets for ~O(1) lookup.
techniques: Separate chaining; Open addressing; Rolling hash; Counting; Memoization
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If need O(1) lookup → Hash table, Hash set.' Most common DS in interviews.

name: Hash Set
definition: Unordered set of unique keys backed by a hash function.
techniques: Duplicate detection; Set intersection; Visited tracking
notes: Default visited-tracker in DFS/BFS.

leetcode.title: Find All Duplicates in an Array
Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array

name: Bloom Filter
definition: Probabilistic set with no false negatives and tunable false positives, using k hash functions.
notes: System design favourite — caches, DB membership checks.

leetcode.title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

name: Binary Tree
definition: Tree where each node has at most two children (left, right).
techniques: DFS (pre/in/post-order); BFS (level order); Recursion; Morris traversal
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If given a tree → DFS, BFS.'

leetcode.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree

name: Binary Search Tree (BST)
definition: Binary tree where left subtree < node < right subtree, giving O(log n) search if balanced.
time: Search/Insert/Delete avg O(log n), worst O(n)
techniques: In-order traversal yields sorted sequence; Validation via bounds
notes: Watch for degenerate skewed BST → use self-balancing in practice.

leetcode.title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree

name: AVL Tree
definition: Self-balancing BST where heights of children differ by at most 1; rebalances via rotations.
notes: Conceptual — interview-relevant for 'how does std::map work?' style questions.

leetcode.title: Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree

name: Red-Black Tree
definition: Self-balancing BST using node colours and 5 invariants to guarantee O(log n).
notes: Backs Java TreeMap, C++ std::map.

leetcode.title: Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree

name: B-Tree / B+ Tree
definition: Multi-way balanced search tree optimized for block-based storage (databases, filesystems).
notes: System design / database internals topic.

leetcode.title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
definition: Tree where each path from root encodes a string; O(L) insert/lookup for length-L key.
techniques: Prefix matching; Autocomplete; Word search backtracking
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for common strings → Map, Trie.'

leetcode.title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

name: Heap (Binary Heap)
definition: Complete binary tree satisfying heap property (min or max at root); array-backed.
techniques: Sift up/down; Heapify; Top-K; Heap sort
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for top/least K items → Heap, Quickselect, Bucket sort.'

leetcode.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
Kth Largest Element in a Stream

name: Priority Queue
definition: Abstract queue ordered by priority; typically a binary heap underneath.
techniques: Dijkstra; Median of stream; Merge K sorted
notes: Java PriorityQueue, Python heapq.

leetcode.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array

name: Segment Tree
definition: Tree over array intervals supporting range queries and point/range updates.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If need range sum/frequency queries → Prefix sum, Binary indexed tree, Segment tree.'

leetcode.title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self

name: Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree)
definition: Compact array structure giving prefix sums with O(log n) update and query.
notes: Lighter than segment tree when only prefix sums needed.

leetcode.title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self

name: Disjoint Set Union (Union-Find)
definition: Partition of elements into disjoint sets supporting near-O(1) union and find.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for connectivity/grouping → Union-Find, DFS.'

leetcode.title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph

name: Suffix Array / Suffix Tree
definition: Sorted array (or compressed tree) of all suffixes of a string; powers fast substring queries.
time: Build O(n log n) · Query O(m + log n)
notes: Advanced; rare in standard interviews but common in competitive programming.

leetcode.title: Number of Ways to Separate Numbers
Sum of Scores of Built Strings

name: Graph
definition: Set of vertices (nodes) and edges; can be directed/undirected, weighted/unweighted, cyclic/acyclic.
space: Adj list O(V+E) · Adj matrix O(V^2)
techniques: BFS; DFS; Union-Find; Topological sort; Dijkstra; Bellman-Ford
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If given a graph → DFS, BFS, Union-Find.'

leetcode.title: Min Cost to Connect All Points

name: Matrix (Grid)
definition: 2D array often treated as an implicit graph; cells = nodes, adjacency = neighbours.
techniques: BFS; DFS; DP on grid; Flood fill
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If given a matrix → BFS, DFS, Dynamic programming.'

name: Monotonic Stack
definition: Stack whose elements are kept in monotonic order; used for next-greater/smaller-element problems.
techniques: Next greater / smaller element; Histogram largest rectangle
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for next greater/smaller element → Monotonic stack.'

name: Monotonic Queue / Deque
definition: Deque maintained in monotonic order so the front is always the max/min of the current window.
notes: LeetCode 239 is the canonical use-case.

leetcode.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K
Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit

name: LRU Cache
definition: Cache that evicts the least-recently-used entry; canonical implementation is hash map + doubly linked list.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If given a stream of data → Heap, Design.' Classic design question.

name: Skip List
definition: Probabilistic data structure of sorted linked lists with multiple levels giving O(log n) ops.
notes: Redis sorted sets are built on skip lists.

leetcode.title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

name: Sparse Table
definition: Precomputed table that answers immutable range min/max/gcd queries in O(1) after O(n log n) prep.
time: Build O(n log n) · Query O(1)
notes: Range min/max query specialty.

leetcode.title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self

name: Linear Search
definition: Scan the collection element-by-element until target is found.
notes: Baseline to beat.

name: Binary Search
definition: Repeatedly halve a sorted range to locate a target in O(log n).
techniques: Two pointers (lo/hi); Binary search on answer; Lower/upper bound
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If input array is sorted → Binary search, Two pointers.' Also 'binary search on answer' pattern.

leetcode.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Peak Index in a Mountain Array
Find Smallest Letter Greater Than Target

name: Ternary Search
definition: Search a unimodal function by splitting into thirds.
notes: Useful for finding extrema of unimodal functions.

leetcode.title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array

name: Quickselect
definition: Partition-based selection of the k-th smallest element in average O(n).
notes: Often beats a heap for one-shot top-K.

leetcode.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
Find Kth Largest XOR Coordinate Value

name: Bubble Sort
definition: Repeatedly swap adjacent out-of-order pairs until sorted.
notes: Educational only.

name: Insertion Sort
definition: Build the sorted list one element at a time by inserting into the correct position.
notes: Good for tiny or nearly-sorted arrays.

name: Selection Sort
definition: Repeatedly select the minimum from the unsorted suffix and place it.
notes: Educational only.

name: Merge Sort
definition: Divide-and-conquer sort: split, recursively sort halves, then merge.
techniques: Divide & conquer; Stable; Used for merging sorted lists
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked to merge sorted lists/intervals → Merge sort, Heap.'

name: Quick Sort
definition: Pick a pivot, partition into smaller/greater, recurse; average O(n log n).
time: Avg O(n log n), worst O(n^2)
notes: Backbone of std::sort in many libraries (often introsort).

leetcode.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array

name: Heap Sort
definition: Build a max-heap, then repeatedly extract the max.
notes: In-place O(n log n) but not stable.

leetcode.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array

name: Counting Sort
definition: Non-comparison sort using counts per value; O(n+k) for small integer range k.
notes: Beats O(n log n) when range is small.

name: Radix Sort
definition: Non-comparison sort: process digits/bits from least to most significant using stable counting sort.
notes: Good for fixed-width integers and strings.

name: Bucket Sort
definition: Distribute elements into buckets, sort each, then concatenate.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for top/least K → Heap, Quickselect, Bucket sort.' Used in Top-K Frequent.

name: Topological Sort
definition: Linear ordering of a DAG's vertices such that for every edge u→v, u precedes v.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for ordering/scheduling → Topological sort.'

leetcode.title: Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix

name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
definition: Level-by-level graph traversal using a queue; finds shortest path in unweighted graphs.
notes: Shortest path in unweighted graph; level-order on trees.

leetcode.title: Average of Levels in Binary Tree

name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
definition: Explore as far as possible along each branch before backtracking; recursion or stack.
notes: Foundation of cycle detection, connectivity, topological sort.

name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
definition: Single-source shortest paths in weighted graph with non-negative edges using a priority queue.
notes: Standard for weighted shortest paths when no negative edges.

name: Bellman-Ford
definition: Single-source shortest paths allowing negative weights; detects negative cycles.
notes: Use when negative weights are possible.

leetcode.title: Find the City With the Smallest Number of Neighbors at a Threshold Distance

name: Floyd-Warshall
definition: All-pairs shortest paths via DP over intermediate vertices.
notes: Good when V is small (≤500) and you need all pairs.

leetcode.title: Find the City With the Smallest Number of Neighbors at a Threshold Distance

name: A* Search
definition: Best-first search using f(n) = g(n) + h(n); admissible heuristic yields optimal path.
time: Depends on heuristic; O(E) at best
notes: Pathfinding in games and AI.

leetcode.title: Find the City With the Smallest Number of Neighbors at a Threshold Distance

name: Kruskal's MST
definition: Build minimum spanning tree by sorting edges and adding via union-find.
notes: MST when edge list is given.

leetcode.title: Find Critical and Pseudo-Critical Edges in Minimum Spanning Tree
Min Cost to Connect All Points
Maximize Spanning Tree Stability with Upgrades

name: Prim's MST
definition: Grow MST by repeatedly adding the cheapest edge to the growing component.
notes: MST when adjacency-list is given.

leetcode.title: Find Critical and Pseudo-Critical Edges in Minimum Spanning Tree
Min Cost to Connect All Points
Maximize Spanning Tree Stability with Upgrades

name: Tarjan's SCC
definition: Find strongly connected components in a directed graph in O(V+E) using DFS lowlinks.
notes: Advanced; useful for 2-SAT and dependency analysis.

leetcode.title: Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix

name: Kosaraju's SCC
definition: Find SCCs with two DFS passes: original graph then reversed graph.
notes: Simpler than Tarjan to implement.

leetcode.title: Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix

name: Floyd's Cycle Detection (Tortoise & Hare)
definition: Detect a cycle in a linked list / functional graph using two pointers at different speeds.
notes: Canonical 'fast & slow pointers' pattern.

name: Dynamic Programming
definition: Solve problems by combining solutions to overlapping subproblems; top-down (memo) or bottom-up (table).
techniques: Memoization; Tabulation; State design; Optimal substructure
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for max/min subarray/subset → DP, Sliding window.'

name: Knapsack (0/1)
definition: Choose items with weights and values to maximize value under a weight cap; each item once.
notes: Subset sum, partition equal subset sum are reductions.

name: Unbounded Knapsack
definition: Knapsack variant allowing unlimited copies of each item.
notes: Coin change is a special case.

name: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
definition: Find longest sequence appearing in both strings in the same order (not necessarily contiguous).
notes: Edit distance variant.

name: Longest Increasing Subsequence (LIS)
definition: Find the longest strictly increasing subsequence; O(n log n) with patience sorting.
notes: Russian doll envelopes is a 2D variant.

name: Edit Distance (Levenshtein)
definition: Minimum number of insert/delete/replace operations to transform one string into another.
notes: Foundational string DP.

name: Matrix Chain Multiplication
definition: Find the cheapest way to parenthesize a chain of matrix multiplications.
notes: Classic interval DP teaching example.

name: Bitmask DP
definition: DP where state encodes a subset as bits in an integer; common for TSP-like problems on small n.

name: Greedy Algorithm
definition: Build a solution by always taking the locally optimal choice; works when the problem has the greedy-choice property.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If need to count/divide optimally → Greedy, DP.'

name: Interval Scheduling
definition: Select the maximum number of non-overlapping intervals; sort by end time and scan.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for overlapping intervals → Sorting, Sweep line.'

leetcode.title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons

name: Huffman Coding
definition: Build optimal prefix code by repeatedly merging two least-frequent symbols.
notes: Compression classic.

name: Divide & Conquer
definition: Solve by splitting problem into subproblems, recursing, and combining results.
notes: Powers merge sort, quick sort, FFT, Karatsuba.

leetcode.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array

name: Backtracking
definition: Build a solution incrementally and undo choices that lead to dead ends.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for all permutations/subsets → Backtracking.'

name: N-Queens
definition: Place N non-attacking queens on an N×N board via backtracking with column/diagonal pruning.
notes: Canonical backtracking exercise.

leetcode.title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number

name: Permutations / Combinations / Subsets
definition: Enumerate all permutations, combinations, or subsets of a collection.
notes: Pattern: choose / explore / unchoose.

name: KMP (Knuth-Morris-Pratt)
definition: Linear-time substring search using a failure function (LPS array).
notes: Substring search and longest proper prefix/suffix problems.

leetcode.title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

name: Rabin-Karp
definition: Substring search using rolling hash; great for multiple patterns.
notes: Use double hashing to avoid collisions.

leetcode.title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

name: Z Algorithm
definition: Compute Z-array (length of longest substring starting at i matching a prefix) in O(n).
notes: Alternative to KMP.

leetcode.title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

name: Manacher's Algorithm
definition: Find all palindromic substrings in linear time.
notes: Longest palindromic substring in O(n).

name: Aho-Corasick
definition: Multi-pattern matching using a trie augmented with failure links.
notes: Antivirus signature scanning; multi-pattern matching.

leetcode.title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

name: Euclidean Algorithm (GCD)
definition: Compute greatest common divisor via gcd(a,b) = gcd(b, a mod b).
notes: Extended Euclidean also computes Bézout coefficients.

name: Sieve of Eratosthenes
definition: Find all primes up to N by iteratively marking multiples.
notes: Linear sieve variant runs in O(N).

name: Fast Exponentiation
definition: Compute a^n in O(log n) via repeated squaring; works on integers and matrices.
notes: Matrix exponentiation accelerates linear recurrences.

name: Bit Manipulation
definition: Solve problems using bitwise AND/OR/XOR/shift tricks.
techniques: XOR pairing; n & (n-1) lowest-bit; Bitmasks; Brian Kernighan's
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked to count bits or use XOR → Bit manipulation.'

name: Two Pointers
definition: Use two indices moving through an array/string in coordinated fashion; opposite ends or same direction.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If input sorted / given a linked list → Two pointers.'

leetcode.title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted

name: Sliding Window
definition: Maintain a window over a contiguous range that grows/shrinks while tracking aggregate state.
techniques: Fixed window; Variable window; Hash map state
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for max/min subarray/subset → DP, Sliding window.'

name: Fast & Slow Pointers
definition: Two pointers advancing at different speeds — detects cycles, finds middle, etc.
techniques: Floyd's cycle detection; Find middle of LL
notes: Linked list cycle problems.

name: Prefix Sum
definition: Precompute cumulative sums so any range sum is answered in O(1).
techniques: 1D and 2D prefix; Hash-map prefix for subarray sums
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If need range sum/frequency queries → Prefix sum, BIT, Segment tree.'

name: Difference Array
definition: Apply range updates in O(1) and reconstruct array in O(n) via prefix-sum.
notes: Inverse of prefix sum for range updates.

leetcode.title: Max Sum of Rectangle No Larger Than K

name: Sweep Line
definition: Sort events by coordinate then sweep, maintaining active set; common for interval problems.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If asked for overlapping intervals → Sorting, Sweep line.'

name: Recursion
definition: Solve a problem by reducing it to smaller instances of itself.
techniques: Base case; Recursive case; Tail recursion
notes: Foundation of DP, backtracking, tree/graph traversal.

name: Memoization
definition: Cache results of subproblem calls to avoid recomputation; top-down DP.
notes: Python's functools.lru_cache; manual map otherwise.

name: Flood Fill
definition: Fill connected region in a grid by recursively visiting neighbours of same value.
notes: Number of islands, surrounded regions.

name: Reservoir Sampling
definition: Sample k items uniformly from a stream of unknown length.
notes: Sean Prashad: 'If given a stream of data → Heap, Design.'

name: Fisher-Yates Shuffle
definition: Uniformly random in-place shuffle in O(n).
notes: Used in randomized quicksort and 'shuffle an array' problem.

name: Boyer-Moore Majority Vote
definition: Find majority element (>n/2) in O(n) time and O(1) space.
notes: Generalizes to k candidates for >n/k threshold.

name: Kadane's Algorithm
definition: Find maximum sum contiguous subarray in O(n) by tracking best ending here / best so far.
notes: Foundational subarray DP.
